subject: LU-1191 nfsd4: Remove check for a 32-bit cookie

messages.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/5449/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.2/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/c39916c0-74dd-11e1-bfc6-5254004bbbd3).  Ran 13 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Actually we don't patch our clients, so it's pointless to add patches for this purpose. We just need to record this info in release notes and hope that upstream distros will pick these patches themselves.

Patch Set 1:

> Oleg Drokin		Mar 25
> Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
> Actually we don't patch our clients, so it's pointless to add patches for this purpose. We just need to record this info in release notes and hope that upstream distros will pick these patches themselves.


In fact, such patch has been picked by new kernels. What we can do is to supply a solution to the Lustre user who want to re-export Lustre through NFSv4 against old kernel.

Since re-export Lustre through NFS is relative seldom case compared with using native Lustre client, it is reasonable to ask the Lustre users to patch the NFSv4 server for such purpose.

So my suggestion is that: only add the patch under our patch directory (do not add it to serise), and add changelog entry to tell Lustre users which patch(s) should be used if they want to re-export Lustre through NFS. Then the Lustre users can patch the kernel as needed by themselves.

parents.subject: LU-1218 proc: Recovery timer in proc always displays 0

commit.subject: LU-1191 nfsd4: Remove check for a 32-bit cookie
commit.message:
LU-1191 nfsd4: Remove check for a 32-bit cookie

Fan Yong <yong.fan@whamcloud.com> noticed setting
FMODE_32bithash wouldn't work with nfsd v4, as
nfsd4_readdir() checks for 32 bit cookies. However, according to
RFC 3530 cookies have a 64 bit type and cookies are also defined
as u64 in 'struct nfsd4_readdir'. So remove the test for >32-bit
values.

Signed-off-by: Bernd Schubert <bernd.schubert@itwm.fraunhofer.de>
Signed-off-by: J. Bruce Fields <bfields@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I6ca802c2c6e2fed6828722d38046bf897513bfc8
